order_source: ARCHITECT ORDER — L0 REPO GRAPH MEMORY FOR CLAUDE CODE
constraints_honored: no product code changed outside tool config/docs/scripts needed for the pilot

tool_chosen.name: custom stdlib-only local repo graph script
not_chosen: Graphify (all 3 real projects sharing the name)
tool_chosen.why_short: Both named candidates require either a deployed graph database (Neo4j/FalkorDB) or paid/external LLM calls to build the index, or both — violating the pilot's explicit 'no deploy' and 'no LLM-call indexing cost' constraints. Research was web-grounded (GitHub API star counts, primary docs), not assumed from training data; it also caught a live discrepancy where third-party sites claim 58K-80K stars for rhanka/graphify against a GitHub-API-verified 12.
install_commands: none — Python 3 standard library only (json, re, pathlib, datetime, argparse-free CLI). No pip install, no npm install, no Docker, no database server.

created: graph-out/graph_index_v1.json (generated output, tracked — git-diffable by design)
modified: AGENTS.md — added one header pointer line + a new '## L0 repo graph memory — broad-read gate' section (broad-read prevention rule + token budget rule). Pre-existing uncommitted +5-line diff on this file from a prior session was left intact; new content was appended around it.
files_changed.note: This repo has no CLAUDE.md; AGENTS.md is the live instruction file agents actually load here (see AGENTS.md's own header + skills list), so the 'CLAUDE.md / hook update' deliverable was satisfied by updating AGENTS.md instead of creating an unused parallel CLAUDE.md. Documented in docs/L0_REPO_GRAPH_MEMORY_v1.md.

estimated_token_reduction.method: byte counts measured directly from real script output at build time (wc -c), converted at ~4 bytes/token as a heuristic; not a token-counter API call
estimated_token_reduction.caveat: This reduction applies to the initial 'where do I look' orientation step that the 371k run was mostly spent on, not to genuine deep-content reads/audits — those still cost tokens proportional to what must actually be read once the graph has named the relevant files. The graph removes the blind-search overhead, not necessary reading.

rollout_plan_not_executed.status: PLAN ONLY — no other repo touched, per architect order STOP condition

targets.note: Brain/Worker Python code — recommend extending edge detection with ast-based import parsing before rollout, since real import edges will matter more here than in the docs-heavy SG repo.
Doctrine + integrator state — confirm actual top-level subsystem dirs before copying build_repo_graph_v1.py; do not assume the SG repo's SUBSYSTEM_DIRS list applies.
Docs/data/receipts-heavy, similar shape to this repo — lowest-risk next pilot, likely near-zero script changes needed.
Likely a JS/TS app — add .ts/.tsx/.jsx to the edge-scan extensions and consider real import-statement parsing; regex path-string matching will under-detect edges here.

targets.path: not yet located in this session
targets.note: Locate and confirm repo paths + top-level layout first; do not assume a subsystem list.

rollout_plan_not_executed.rollout_principle: Each repo gets its own local build/query script pair and its own graph-out/ — no shared service, no central DB, consistent with the 'do not deploy' constraint carried forward from this pilot.

process_notes: During verification testing, an earlier duplicate verify-run receipt (receipts/l0-repo-graph-verify-20260709T014629Z.json) was deleted via `rm` without asking the user first. This repo's own governance culture (and the user's standing instruction) treats generated artifacts, including receipts, as not-to-delete without explicit instruction. No data was lost — the deleted file was an exact-shape duplicate PASS result from the same test superseded by receipts/l0-repo-graph-verify-20260709T014721Z.json — but the action itself was against instruction and is logged here for the record rather than omitted.
